task:
ATOM 1 of ticket 0039 — DIAGNOSE the ~6s wait between a launch click (POST /runs) and the cmux session pane actually appearing, and produce committable timing instrumentation that yields per-stage evidence on every future launch.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Trace the full launch path and enumerate, in order, every step from `launchRun` entry (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts) through run creation and the runner's session spawn, into `CmuxSessionHost.spawn` (packages/session-hosts/src/cmux/driver.ts). For EACH step note whether it does I/O (git, fs, an `execFile('cmux', …)` subprocess round-trip, app-launch poll) and its expected cost. Pay specific attention to: (a) `#ensureHost` cold-launching cmux (`open -a cmux` + 1000ms poll loop up to 15s) when the socket isn't already up; (b) the count of SERIAL cmux CLI subprocess calls per spawn (new-workspace, list-pane-surfaces, rename-tab, send, send-key, focus-pane) and that this happens once per persona spawned (Oscar, Bob, possibly Deb); (c) the pre-spawn stale-daemon git check and run-input assembly (reads persona/standards/play files).

2. Add LIGHTWEIGHT, durable timing instrumentation so the dominant cost is provable from real launches, not asserted. Use the existing event spine: `ctx.store.recordEvent({ runId, type: '…', data: { ms, … } })` in launcher.ts at the key seams (launch entry, run-created, spawn-start/spawn-end per persona), and in the cmux driver record per-CLI-call durations (e.g. a single aggregated `data: { calls: [{cmd, ms}], totalMs }` per spawn) — inject a clock/now so it stays unit-testable, do NOT call Date.now() inline in a way that breaks existing tests. Keep it small and behavior-preserving: instrumentation only, NO change to launch ordering or timing yet.

3. Write the diagnosis as a findings note at `cocoder/runs/60-run_204/diagnosis-0039.md` (create the dir): the ordered step list with costs, the identified dominant contributor(s) to the ~6s, and a clear recommendation of which ticket branch applies — (a) intentional built-in delay to justify/remove, (b) a genuine fixable inefficiency (e.g. serialized cmux calls that could be parallelized/reduced, or a redundant cold-launch poll), or (c) the delay is irreducible and warrants a progress modal in Oz. If (b), name the specific calls that can be parallelized or elided.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A code-traced, ordered breakdown of the launch path with per-step cost reasoning exists in the diagnosis note, with an explicit dominant-cost verdict and an a/b/c recommendation.
- New timing instrumentation emits structured events at the seams above and compiles + typechecks; if any cmux/launcher/runner test asserts emitted events or call counts, it still passes (update tests only to ACCOMMODATE new events, never to weaken an existing assertion).
- `pnpm -w typecheck` (or the package-scoped equivalent) is green and the relevant package test suite passes. Report the exact commands run and their exit codes.

MUST NOT BREAK: launch ordering, spawn behavior, teardown, or any existing event/test contract. This atom changes NOTHING about how fast the launch is — it only measures it and writes the diagnosis. The fix (branch b or the modal of branch c) is a separate follow-up atom gated on your findings.

ATOM 2 of ticket 0039 (branch c — the founder-visible deliverable; ticket title is 'Launch Status in Oz Dashboard'): add a launch-progress modal to the Oz dashboard so the founder sees live status during the multi-second launch instead of staring at nothing, and it auto-closes when the launch is up. Drive it from the launch lifecycle events Atom 1 already emits (verified present in RunDetail.events).

CONTEXT / ANCHORS:
- Launch handlers: packages/ui/src/renderer/App.tsx — `doLaunch` (~line 536), `handleLaunch`, `doLaunchTicket`/`handleLaunchTicket`, and the resume path in `handleRunAction('retry')`. Today they call `launchRun`/`launchTicketRun` (packages/ui/src/renderer/live.ts), get a 202 with `{runId}` immediately (panes appear seconds later), and just fire a toast via `notify`.
- Event source: `loadRunDetail(oz, runId, names)` returns `RunDetail` with `sessions: RunSession[]` and `events: RunEvent[]` ({type, data, at}). The relevant launch events (emitted by Atom 1) are: `launch-entry`, `launch-run-input-assembled`, `launch-stale-check-finished`, `launch-run-created`, `launch-spawn-start` {persona,label}, `launch-spawn-end` {persona,ok,ms,message?}, and `cmux-spawn-timing`. Sessions populate as personas spawn.
- Reuse the existing `Modal` primitive from packages/ui/src/renderer/ui/primitives.tsx (see how sections/modals.tsx and PriorityDetailModal.tsx use it).

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. On a LIVE launch click (priority, ticket, ad-hoc, and resume), OPEN the modal immediately with a determinate 'Launching “<name>”…' heading and a spinner — before/right as the POST resolves, so there is no dead air.
2. After the 202 returns a runId, poll `loadRunDetail(runId)` on a short cadence (~500–800ms) WHILE THE MODAL IS OPEN, and render staged progress derived from `events`: e.g. run created → 'Starting Oscar…' on Oscar's `launch-spawn-start` → 'Oscar ready' on its `launch-spawn-end {ok:true}`, and likewise for Bob/Deb. Keep the copy plain-English and derived from the events, not hard-coded timings.
3. AUTO-CLOSE the modal when the launch is up: when Oscar's session is present in `sessions` (its pane exists) or Oscar's `launch-spawn-end {ok:true}` has arrived (founder can now watch the pane). Stop the poll on close.
4. ERROR PATH — do NOT silently dismiss: if `launchRun` returns non-2xx (surface the existing 409 'already in flight' wording and other error text), or a `launch-spawn-end {ok:false}` / `run-error` / failed status arrives, keep the modal open showing the reason with a manual Close button.
5. Preserve existing behavior elsewhere: the NON-live (seed) path must still route launch to the chat message (`onSend(...)`) with NO modal; keep `refreshActiveWs()` after a successful launch; don't regress the toast semantics for the error/409 cases (they can now live inside the modal, but the non-live path is unchanged).

WRITE SCOPE: packages/ui/** only. Do NOT change daemon/runner/session-host launch behavior or timing in this atom — this is purely the Oz status surface consuming events that already exist.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Clicking Launch in live mode opens the modal immediately; it shows staged progress sourced from RunDetail.events and auto-closes once Oscar's pane/session is up; on a launch error it stays open with the reason and a Close control (no silent dismiss).
- Non-live (seed) launch path is unchanged (still routes to chat; no modal).
- A new/updated renderer (jsdom) test covers: modal opens on launch, advances as launch events arrive, auto-closes when Oscar is up, and stays open with the message on the error path. Tests MUST use the seed/fake bridge — never trigger a real POST /runs.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui typecheck` is green and the @cocoder/ui test suite passes. Report the exact commands run and their exit codes.

MUST NOT BREAK: the non-live chat-routed launch, the 409/error handling, existing renderer tests, or any other dashboard surface. No change to launch speed — this atom only surfaces status; the optional branch-(b) cmux-call optimization is a separate follow-up gated on real `cmux-spawn-timing` numbers.
